statusLine: EU fuel security is under strain with 13 member states critically low on multiple fuel types and oil prices up 6.7% to $107.94/barrel amid ongoing supply disruptions.
fullAnalysis:
The EU faces significant fuel security challenges with widespread critical shortages across member states. While EU averages show petrol at 94.5 days and diesel at 95.8 days—just above the 90-day mandatory minimum—these figures mask severe disparities. Thirteen countries are in critical status for petrol reserves, with Latvia holding only 29.3 days and Lithuania at 39.3 days. The diesel situation is equally concerning, with Romania critically low at just 20.2 days and Poland at 71 days.

Jet fuel presents the most acute risk, averaging only 89.9 days EU-wide and falling below the safety threshold. Poland's jet fuel reserves are critically depleted at 21.3 days, while Portugal holds just 23.1 days. Only Belgium maintains adequate jet fuel stocks at 97.6 days, highlighting the vulnerability of European aviation fuel supplies.

The recent 6.7% spike in Brent crude to $107.94/barrel—representing a 27% increase since Middle East conflicts began—compounds these supply concerns. Rising fuel prices are evident across the EU, with petrol averaging €1.789/L and diesel at €2/L. The Netherlands faces the highest petrol prices at €2.334/L, while strategic reserve releases have provided only temporary relief from the underlying supply constraints affecting multiple member states.
keyPoints: 13 EU countries have critical petrol reserves, with Latvia at only 29.3 days and Lithuania at 39.3 days supply
Romania's diesel reserves are critically low at 20.2 days, while EU jet fuel averages 89.9 days—below the 90-day minimum
Brent crude jumped 6.7% to $107.94/barrel, marking a 27% increase since Middle East supply disruptions began
Only Germany and Czechia maintain safe petrol reserves above 90 days, while most major economies face critical shortages
Fuel prices vary dramatically across the EU, from Malta's €1.34/L petrol to Netherlands' €2.334/L amid supply stress
